text: Does the bright NLS soliton on the compound-soliton manifold M_cs survive long-time propagation under B-NLS (kappa=+1) up to T=8.0 with diagnostics: N peak >= 1.125, mass drift <5%, |u|,|N|,|phi|<25, ||m||/||N phi_x|| <0.2 ?

ic: Bright soliton: N(x,0)=A^2 sech^2(A(x+5)) with A=1.5; phi(x,0)=0.5 x; u(x,0)=0.5 N (on M_cs)
method: Direct (u, N, phi) Fourier pseudospectral on periodic [-15,15] with Nx=256, explicit RK4. Q with eps=1e-12 floor. dt=1e-3.

interpretation: Catastrophic blow-up at step 1. Initially diagnosed as 1/sqrt(N) amplification, but on closer look ALSO the non-periodic phi(x,0)=0.5*x violates Fourier periodicity, producing spurious phi_x oscillations of ~80 at the boundary (vs the physically-correct 0.5). These boundary spikes propagate immediately into phi_xt and explode u_t. Two coupled issues: (a) non-periodic phi IC, (b) small-N tails in quantum potential.

rationale: Two issues identified. Address one at a time per progressive-complexity discipline. The dominant one is the non-periodic phi IC, but I (the agent) addressed the N-floor first in E2. Recording E2 as run.

method: E1 + N-floor regularization of Q: replace sqrt(N) by sqrt(N+eps_N) with eps_N=1e-6. All else identical.
bank_use_rationale: NoKB; single change addressing the 1/sqrt(N) failure mode initially attributed to F1.

interpretation: Same step-1 blow-up. N-floor did NOT help. Closer probe revealed the dominant failure is the non-periodic phi(x,0)=0.5*x: the Fourier derivative of a non-periodic function produces Gibbs oscillations of ~80 at boundaries (vs the correct 0.5 in the interior), which immediately corrupts phi_x, then phi_xt drives u_t to ~1e16 in one RK4 step. The N-floor alone cannot fix this; we need a periodic representation of phi. Fix: shift phi -> psi where psi = phi - v*x with v=0.5 (the constant phi_x boost). Then psi is periodic (in fact psi(x,0)=0), and phi_x = psi_x + v is computed correctly. The output requires phi, so we reconstruct phi = psi + v*x. This is a periodicity-compatible representation, one cohesive single change vs E2.

rationale: Dominant failure mode is non-periodic phi IC, not 1/sqrt(N). Switch to tracking psi = phi - 0.5*x (periodic, since phi-0.5*x = 0 at t=0). One representation change. Keep the N-floor too as a cheap safety on Q (already in place from E2). E3 = E2 + (phi -> psi periodic representation).

ic: same as E1, but encode phi(x,0)=0.5*x as psi(x,0)=0 with constant boost v=0.5
method: Periodic Fourier pseudospectral RK4 with phase variable psi = phi - 0.5*x. phi_x = psi_x + 0.5. All N, u equations unchanged (they only use phi_x). For output, reconstruct phi = psi + 0.5*x. Q computed with N-floor eps_N=1e-6. dt=1e-3. One representation change over E2.
bank_use_rationale: NoKB; single representation change to make phi compatible with Fourier periodicity.
